text.title: Evaluation Process — Offline, A/B, and Production
text.summary: Step-by-step evaluation workflow: offline dataset setup, A/B testing, production monitoring thresholds, and quality gates.

rule.statement: Build your evaluation dataset from real production queries, not synthetic ones; minimum 200 samples; version it alongside model/prompt versions.
rule.rationale: Synthetic datasets miss the long tail of real user behavior. Mismatched dataset versions make cross-evaluation comparisons meaningless.

list.item: Define evaluation goals: model selection, prompt optimization, or monitoring baseline
Prepare test dataset: min 100 samples for significance (200+ recommended), balanced across categories, no overlap with training data
Set temperature=0 for reproducibility across runs
Use same prompts across all models being compared
Log all inputs, outputs, token counts, and latency per sample

rule.statement: Define success metrics and calculate required sample size before traffic splitting; run until p < 0.05 and check for novelty effects.
rule.rationale: Underpowered A/B tests produce false positives that lead to wrong model selection decisions.

list.item: Define hypothesis and primary success metric before starting
Calculate sample size for desired statistical power
Set up 50/50 or staged rollout traffic split with full tracking
Monitor guardrail metrics: errors, latency, safety scores
Analyze with statistical significance (p < 0.05) and segment by user type

rule.statement: Sample 1–10% of production traffic for quality evaluation; run LLM-as-judge on 0.1–1%; set cost alerts at 2x baseline and page at 5x.
rule.rationale: Full evaluation on 100% of production traffic is cost-prohibitive; sampling provides sufficient signal. Cost alerts catch runaway agent loops before they drain budget.

list.item: Daily: check error rates and p95 latency
Weekly: review sampled outputs for quality regression
Monthly: run comprehensive evaluation against baseline
Quarterly: refresh test datasets to match current query distribution
Error rate: warning > 1%, critical > 5%
p95 latency: warning > 3s, critical > 10s
Empty responses: warning > 0.5%, critical > 2%
Quality score: warning < 4.0/5.0, critical < 3.5/5.0

rule.statement: Block deployment if quality drops >5% on core metrics versus the production baseline.
rule.rationale: Catching quality regressions in CI prevents silent degradation in production.

list.item: Before staging: all automated tests pass, quality meets threshold, latency within SLA, no safety issues
Before production: staging evaluation complete, A/B positive, rollback plan documented, dashboards ready

rule.statement: Evaluation agents must rate-limit concurrent LLM calls and must not cache LLM-as-judge results across prompt versions.
rule.rationale: Parallel evaluation of 1000 samples at 50 concurrent calls hits API rate limits. Stale evaluations from a prior prompt version corrupt cross-version comparisons.
